skill.name: create-pr
skill.description: Create pull requests that follow Sentry review, title, description, and issue reference conventions.
skill.summary: Guides Sentry-style pull request preparation, descriptions, titles, reviewers, and issue links.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many command and weak-crypto patterns in SKILL.md. Review found the weak-crypto alerts are false positives from Markdown text, while git and GitHub CLI commands are intentional for pull request creation. The skill is publishable with a warning because it can create remote GitHub pull requests and request reviewers.

medium_findings.title: Repository Commands Can Change Remote GitHub State
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to run git inspection commands and GitHub CLI commands. This is expected for creating pull requests, but it can publish PRs and request reviewers if used without user confirmation.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicitly shown in shell blocks and include gh pr create and gh pr edit. They match the skill purpose, so this is operational risk rather than evidence of malicious behavior.

medium_findings.title: Shell Command Substitution In PR Body Example
medium_findings.description: The PR creation example uses command substitution to pass a heredoc into gh pr create. The quoted heredoc limits injection risk, but agents should still avoid inserting untrusted shell syntax into executable commands.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command substitution is visible and executable if copied. The example uses a quoted EOF delimiter and placeholder body text, so no direct exploit or malicious intent is shown.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Documentation Links
low_findings.description: The skill links to Sentry engineering documentation. These are ordinary reference links and do not show data exfiltration or hidden network behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Both URLs are visible Markdown references to Sentry documentation. No code sends credentials, repository data, or user content to those URLs.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported weak cryptographic algorithm patterns, but the referenced lines contain description text, examples, and issue references. No hashing, encryption, or crypto API usage is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is a Markdown instruction guide, and the cited lines do not call cryptographic functions. The alerts appear caused by keyword matches inside normal prose and examples.

dangerous_patterns.title: External CLI Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: The workflow asks an agent to run git and gh commands. Users should review branch state, title, body, and reviewers before allowing commands that create or edit a pull request.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The external commands are direct and intentional. They are not obfuscated, but they can affect remote GitHub resources when executed.

content.value_statement: Teams need clear pull requests that reviewers can understand quickly. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through Sentry-style PR titles, descriptions, reviewers, and issue links.
actual_capabilities: Checks branch status and commit history before PR creation.
Reviews the branch diff against main before writing a description.
Structures PR descriptions around purpose, motivation, alternatives, and reviewer context.
Applies Sentry-style title patterns for feature, fix, and refactor changes.
Creates pull requests with the GitHub CLI when gh is authenticated.
Adds individual or team reviewers with GitHub CLI commands.
limitations: Requires an authenticated GitHub CLI installation.
Assumes the repository uses main as the base branch.
Does not validate CI status, test results, or merge conflicts.
Uses Sentry conventions, which may not match every team workflow.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Reviewable Feature PR
use_cases.description: Create a concise pull request that explains the feature purpose, tradeoffs, and related issue links.

use_cases.title: Document a Bug Fix Clearly
use_cases.description: Turn a bug fix branch into a reviewer-friendly PR with context about the failure and resolution.

use_cases.title: Standardize Team PR Hygiene
use_cases.description: Help contributors follow consistent title, description, reviewer, and issue reference conventions.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic PR
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a normal branch.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the create-pr skill to inspect my branch and draft a Sentry-style pull request description.

prompt_templates.title: Create a PR With Issue Context
prompt_templates.scenario: PR needs issue links and reviewer context.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the create-pr skill. Include the related issue reference, explain why the change is needed, and suggest reviewers if the branch history supports it.

prompt_templates.title: Improve an Existing PR Draft
prompt_templates.scenario: A draft needs clearer reviewer guidance.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the create-pr skill to review my current PR draft. Rewrite it to follow Sentry conventions and remove redundant diff summaries.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Focused Refactor PR
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for a no-behavior-change refactor.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the create-pr skill to prepare a refactor PR. Emphasize behavior stability, explain the future work it enables, and identify areas needing careful review.

output_examples.input: Create a pull request for my branch that adds Slack thread replies for alerts.
output_examples.output: A PR title using the feature format, a short explanation of the change, motivation for threading, alternatives considered, and an issue reference.

output_examples.input: Prepare a bug fix PR for a null user response crash.
output_examples.output: A focused PR description that explains the null response, the crash impact, the fix, and the related issue link.

output_examples.input: Make my refactor PR easier to review.
output_examples.output: A concise description that states there is no behavior change, explains the extracted logic, and highlights review areas.

best_practices: Review generated titles and descriptions before creating the PR.
Confirm the base branch and diff are correct before running GitHub CLI commands.
Keep each PR focused on one feature, fix, or refactor.
anti_patterns: Do not create PRs from branches with unrelated changes.
Do not include long test-plan checklists when the team convention excludes them.
Do not add reviewers before confirming ownership and availability.

faq.question: Does this skill create pull requests automatically?
faq.answer: It provides GitHub CLI commands that can create a PR when executed. Review the command before approving it.

faq.question: Does it require GitHub CLI?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill expects gh to be installed, authenticated, and available in the shell.

faq.question: Can it work outside Sentry repositories?
faq.answer: Yes, but the title, description, and review conventions are based on Sentry practices.

faq.question: Does it run tests before opening a PR?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on PR preparation and does not define test or CI commands.

faq.question: Can it add reviewers?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes examples for adding people or teams with GitHub CLI commands.

faq.question: What should users verify before using it?
faq.answer: Users should verify branch status, diff scope, PR title, body text, and reviewer list.
